purpose: Measured code-coverage floor contract for the hand-written SDK/CLI/MCP surface. Floors are pinned to the honest measured baseline minus a small margin and ratchet monotonically upward; generated wrapper/src/** is excluded by every package vitest config. Live-key sandbox suites contribute no coverage in CI. DUAL AUTHORITY: each package's vitest.config.ts thresholds mirror these floors so a bare `vitest --coverage` enforces locally; this file (via scripts/check-coverage-floor.mjs / `make coverage`) is the canonical cross-package monotonic ratchet. When you raise a floor here, raise the matching vitest.config.ts thresholds in the same commit. NOTE (2026-06-20, Deferred 4b + coverage hardening): cli/mcp moved vitest 2.x -> 4.x so all three packages share one major. The vitest 4 v8 (AST-aware) provider counts functions/branches more granularly than v2, so the v2-calibrated cli/mcp floors were not comparable to v4 totals. Rather than simply rebaseline down, new behavior tests were added for the lowest-covered handler files (cli timeoff/entries/invoices; mcp approvals/audit/tags/customFields/tasks/clients/sharedReports), lifting honest v4 functions ~+7-8pp and branches ~+9-10pp; cli lines/statements now exceed the old v2 floors. Floors below are the honest v4 measured baseline minus a small margin; the monotonic-up ratchet resumes from here. wrapper was already on vitest 4 and is unchanged.
